content: A TV tower has a height $$72 \mathrm{~m}$$. The maximum distance upto which TV transmission can be received is (Take, radius of the earth as $$\left.6.4 \times 10^6 \mathrm{~m}\right)$$
explanation:
The height of TV tower, $$h=72 \mathrm{~m}$$

The maximum distance upto which transmission can be received,

$$d=\sqrt{2 R h}=\sqrt{2 \times 6.4 \times 10^6 \times 72}=30.35 \mathrm{~km}$$

content: Maximum amplitude of message signal in amplitude modulation is 20 cm. If the amplitude of carrier wave is 40 cm, then modulation index of modulated wave is
In detector, output circuit consists of $$R=10~k\Omega$$ and $$C=100~pF$$. The frequency of carrier signal which it can detect is
explanation:
Given, $$R = 10k\Omega = {10^4}\Omega $$

$$C = 100pF = 100 \times {10^{ - 12}}F$$

Time constant of RC circuit,

$$\tau = RC = {10^4} \times 100 \times {10^{ - 12}} = {10^{ - 6}}\,s$$

For demodulation $${1 \over {{f_C}}} < < RC \Rightarrow {f_C} > > {1 \over {RC}}$$

or $${f_C} > > {1 \over {{{10}^{ - 6}}}} = {10^6}\,Hz$$

$$\therefore$$ $${f_C} > > 1\,MHz$$

content: An optical fibre communication system works on a wavelength of 1.3 $$\mu$$m. The number of subscribers it can feed, if a channel requires 20 kHz are
explanation:
Given, $$\mathrm{\lambda=1.3~\mu m=1.3\times10^{-6}~m}$$

Bandwidth of channel, BW = 20 kHz = 20 $$\times$$ 10$$^3$$ Hz

Optical source frequency,

$$f = {c \over \lambda } = {{3 \times {{10}^8}} \over {1.3 \times {{10}^{ - 6}}}}$$

$$ = 2.3 \times {10^{14}}$$ Hz

$$\therefore$$ Number of channels, $$n = {f \over {BW}} = {{2.3 \times {{10}^{14}}} \over {20 \times {{10}^3}}}$$

$$ = 1.15 \times {10^{10}}$$

options.content: less than that of sky waves
greater than that of sky waves
less than that of space waves
equal to that of space waves

explanation: Wavelength of ground waves have frequency greater than the wavelength of sky waves and space waves both.
